run_summary: Screen 1 faces a critical portfolio-management moment: 6 open positions all hit day 12-14 of the holding window with mixed P&L, and today's flag batch (Anthropic Claude Tag + Slack integration) overlaps heavily with existing holdings. PLAB is flagged again with identical threat/panic calibration but already +7.97% — past recovery. CGNX and DOCN are in the portfolio but re-flagged at lower confidence. Three new names (ONTO, AEIS, PHR) merit entry; however, the basket rule applies to the Claude Tag trigger (4 names qualify: ONTO, PLAB re-flag, AEIS, DOCN), forcing exploratory tier. Incumbent positions warrant disciplined exits/holds at the window boundary.

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: Anthropic's Claude Tag brings always-on AI teammate to Slack
entries.thesis: ONTO sells process-control equipment to advanced-node fabs (including AI accelerator manufacturing). A Slack AI integration has zero bearing on wafer inspection demand; if anything, more AI infrastructure buildout means more advanced-node capacity and more demand for ONTO's tools. The sharp drop is pure semi-cap sympathy on an unrelated AI headline.
entries.what_kills: Evidence of AI capex digestion at leading-edge fabs; customer concentration risk materializing (a key customer cutting orders); Rigaku regulatory snag.
entries.reasoning: Passes BUY eligibility: classification=OVERDONE, confidence=4 >= min_buy_confidence=3, threat_assessment='none', panic_calibration='unjustified'. Filings cleanly support the thesis — Risk Factors discuss AI only as peripheral operational risk; no mention of AI competition to semiconductor metrology/inspection. However, BASKET RULE APPLIES: ONTO, PLAB (re-flag), AEIS, and DOCN all clear thresholds from the same Claude Tag catalyst. Four flags from one trigger = basket bet on the reading of Claude Tag as sympathy-only. Basket rule mandates exploratory tier for all four to avoid concentration (conviction sizing would be 60%+ of bankroll on one thesis read). ONTO gets 6% exploratory position to test the thesis.

red_team.weakest_link: The basket-rule logic assumes Claude Tag in Slack is truly a non-event for semiconductor capex, but the five-day weakness (-5.98%) suggests the market is already pricing in something more durable than pure sympathy.
red_team.critique: The bull thesis rests on the claim that Claude Tag has 'zero bearing on wafer inspection demand' and that the drop is 'pure semi-cap sympathy' on an unrelated headline. However, a -5.98% five-day move is not trivial sympathy; it suggests the market may be repricing semiconductor capex expectations more broadly. The thesis correctly notes that more AI infrastructure buildout should support ONTO, but does not address whether the market is instead signaling that AI capex is saturating or that leading-edge node demand is softening. The basket-rule framing (four names from one trigger = exploratory position) is disciplined, but it does not explain why this specific name has held weakness over five days if the trigger is truly exogenous.

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries, Inc
entries.catalyst: Anthropic's Claude Tag brings always-on AI teammate to Slack
entries.thesis: AEIS sells power conversion to both semiconductor fabs and AI data centers. The 10-K explicitly identifies AI as a demand driver they are scaling capacity to meet. A Slack AI integration is mechanically backwards as a bear catalyst — more enterprise AI deployment → more data-center power demand → more AEIS. The drop is pure sympathy.
entries.what_kills: Evidence of data-center buildout pause; semiconductor capex cycle peak signals.
entries.reasoning: Passes BUY eligibility: classification=OVERDONE, confidence=4, threat_assessment='none', panic_calibration='unjustified'. 10-K explicitly identifies AI infrastructure investment as a demand driver; power-conversion sales to data-center buildout are mechanically backward as a bear case for Claude Tag in Slack. However, BASKET RULE APPLIES (see ONTO above). AEIS is the second name in the Claude Tag basket; exploratory tier (6%) maintains discipline around the grouped thesis read.

red_team.weakest_link: The thesis assumes data-center power demand is mechanically independent of the Claude Tag news, but the one-day move (-5.99%) combined with below-average volume (0.84×) suggests thin liquidity and potential forced selling, not a pure sympathy fade.
red_team.critique: AEIS is correct that power conversion for data centers is a structural tailwind separate from Slack integration news. The 10-K framing of AI as an explicit demand driver is material. However, the move occurred on below-average volume (0.84×), which is atypical for a true sympathy fade—sympathy drops usually occur on volume surges. Low-volume weakness on a day when the market is de-risking AI themes more broadly could signal fund redemptions or margin calls rather than a reasoned repricing of AEIS's fundamentals, meaning the bounce may be delayed or incomplete.

entries.name: Phreesia, Inc.
entries.thesis: Agentic swarms target generic enterprise workflow automation; Phreesia's intake/engagement business is gated by HIPAA, EHR/PM integration plumbing, payer-specific eligibility, and life-sciences network contracts. A 'loopy' agent swarm cannot drop into a Cerner/Epic integration or take over patient-payment processing. The sympathy drop on a sub-average-volume day looks thematic, not fundamental.
entries.what_kills: An EHR vendor (Epic/Cerner) announcing native agentic intake; a Phreesia customer-cohort or net-retention deterioration in next print; a new risk-factor amendment naming agentic AI specifically.
entries.reasoning: Passes BUY eligibility: classification=OVERDONE, confidence=3 = min_buy_confidence, threat_assessment='minimal', panic_calibration='unjustified'. Filings acknowledge AI as a general competitive concern but describe moat as HIPAA-gated integrations, EHR/PM plumbing, payer eligibility workflows — not generic automation. Agentic swarms cannot drop into Cerner/Epic integrations or payment processing. Catalyst is 'AI world is getting loopy' (agentic systems), separate trigger from Claude Tag basket. Confidence=3 and threat_assessment='minimal' makes this exploratory tier (not conviction). PHR does not trigger the basket rule because it is a standalone trigger.

red_team.weakest_link: Healthcare workflow moats (HIPAA, EHR integration, payer networks) are correctly identified, but the thesis does not address whether recent agentic AI announcements have already prompted EHR vendors to accelerate their own native agentic features, making the moat less durable than it appears today.
red_team.critique: The bull case correctly notes that agentic swarms cannot easily drop into HIPAA-gated EHR/PM integrations and payment workflows. However, the thesis assumes that moat is static. If Epic, Cerner, or other EHR vendors are accelerating native agentic intake in response to the recent wave of AI headlines, the sympathy drop in PHR could be rational rather than a false-victim pattern. The flag does list 'an EHR vendor announcing native agentic intake' as a what_kills scenario, but does not assess the probability of that outcome in the near term. The low volume (0.7×) on a -4.9% move also suggests thin conviction selling rather than panic, which is consistent with the thesis but does not strengthen it.

entries.name: DigitalOcean Holdings, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Anthropic's Claude Tag brings always-on AI teammate to Slack
entries.thesis: Claude Tag in Slack is an enterprise productivity feature; DOCN sells cloud infrastructure to developers and SMBs. There's no product-overlap mechanism. The five-day drop is already extended, suggesting the move has multiple ingredients (general cloud sympathy on AI capex peak fears), but today's incremental drop on Claude Tag specifically is unjustified.
entries.what_kills: Multi-day 5-day weakness suggests something else may be in play — check for any DOCN-specific guidance issues or hyperscaler price-cut announcements that could pressure DOCN's pricing.
entries.reasoning: Killed by red-team. Weakest link: The five-day move of -13.3% is already extended, and the 6% exploratory position size is misaligned with the confidence level—confidence=3 (minimal threat) does not justify adding capital to a name that has already fallen sharply, especially when the flag itself warns that multi-day weakness suggests other factors are at play. Bear case: The flag explicitly notes: 'multi-day weakness suggests something else may be in play—check for any DOCN-specific guidance issues or hyperscaler price-cut announcements.' This is a red flag that the Claude Tag sympathy story is incomplete. DOCN down 13.3% in five days and down another 5.45% today is not a pattern consistent with a clean, exogenous sympathy fade; it suggests either DOCN-specific deterioration or that the market is repricing cloud infrastructure broadly. Buying a 6% exploratory position into a name with confidence=3 and explicit caution language in the 'what_kills' section is an unforced error. The position size should be much smaller or entry should wait for clarity on the multi-day weakness. (Haiku's original BUY reasoning: Passes BUY eligibility: classification=OVERDONE, confidence=3 = min_buy_confidence, threat_assessment='minimal', panic_calibration='unjustified'. Filings show no product-overlap threat; five_day_change_pct=-13.3% suggests move is extended, but today's -5.45% drop is unjustified by Claude Tag product overlap. However, BASKET RULE APPLIES: DOCN is the fourth name in the Claude Tag basket (ONTO, AEIS, DOCN, and the re-flagged PLAB). Exploratory tier (6%) for all basket members. Note: flag notes 'multi-day weakness suggests something else may be in play' — monitor for separate DOCN-specific catalysts post-entry.)

red_team.weakest_link: The five-day move of -13.3% is already extended, and the 6% exploratory position size is misaligned with the confidence level—confidence=3 (minimal threat) does not justify adding capital to a name that has already fallen sharply, especially when the flag itself warns that multi-day weakness suggests other factors are at play.
red_team.critique: The flag explicitly notes: 'multi-day weakness suggests something else may be in play—check for any DOCN-specific guidance issues or hyperscaler price-cut announcements.' This is a red flag that the Claude Tag sympathy story is incomplete. DOCN down 13.3% in five days and down another 5.45% today is not a pattern consistent with a clean, exogenous sympathy fade; it suggests either DOCN-specific deterioration or that the market is repricing cloud infrastructure broadly. Buying a 6% exploratory position into a name with confidence=3 and explicit caution language in the 'what_kills' section is an unforced error. The position size should be much smaller or entry should wait for clarity on the multi-day weakness.
